commands.notes: No open PRs existed before creating release PR #144.
Only origin/main existed before the temporary PR branch was created.
No whitespace errors in the staged release fix.
Backend package tests passed against the local test database.
Blocked by local Windows cgo toolchain: race mode requires cgo, and the available Cygwin gcc is rejected for native Windows builds; MinGW is required.
Frontend production build passed, including menu/i18n/system visual/smoke prechecks.
Full smoke suite passed, covering platform, system pages/forms/authz/governance/API, and business generated module flows.

commands.command: git commit -m "fix: harden data scope and cleanup layout"
commands.notes: Pre-commit reran frontend type-check, ESLint/Prettier, and gofmt before creating commit 3c6b7b3.
GitHub rejected direct main push because repository rules require changes through a pull request.
Created PR #144 for the protected-branch merge path.
Pull-request CI run passed Fast Checks, Unit Tests, Go Lint, and CI Summary.
Pull-request Security Gates passed Workflow Security, CodeQL Security, Dependency Vulnerabilities, Secret Scan, and Security Summary.
Code Quality Gates passed backend tests, frontend contract, Go lint, smoke sanity, and duplication; Docs Governance failed because the initial PR body did not use the repository governance template.

graphChecks.notes: The release fix was scoped by direct diff and existing tests; no symbol-level CodeGraph query was required after the affected files were already isolated.

browserEvidence.notes: The cleanup range UI blocker was reproduced before the fix and passed in the final full smoke run.

runtimeGap: Race-mode backend validation is blocked on this Windows workstation until a MinGW cgo toolchain is available.
knownGaps: go test -race ./backend/... could not run locally because the Windows C toolchain is Cygwin gcc rather than MinGW.
The code-review skill requires independent code-reviewer and architect lanes before approval, but subagent delegation was not available under this session's tool constraints.
The first PR body failed Docs Governance and was corrected by adding this task packet and updating the PR body.
